24.reason: [Error: No NULL check before dereferencing p. If p is NULL (e.g., when a node has only one child), accessing p->left causes segmentation fault.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: No NULL check before dereferencing p. If p is NULL (e.g., when a node has only one child), accessing p->left causes segmentation fault.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  p is not checked for NULL; if p is NULL, this line causes segmentation fault
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing NULL check for p causes segmentation fault when accessing p->left or p->right on a null pointer
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing NULL check for pointer p; when a node has only one child, leafprint is recursively called with NULL, causing a segmentation fault upon accessing p->left.

7.reason: [Using a global variable 'count' to track height is incorrect because it doesn't represent the actual depth of the current node during traversal; height should be passed as a parameter or tracked via recursion stack, not a shared global state.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Using a global variable 'count' to track height is incorrect because it doesn't represent the actual depth of the current node during traversal; height should be passed as a parameter or tracked via recursion stack, not a shared global state.]

26.reason: [Printing 'count' here is wrong because 'count' does not correctly reflect the height of the current leaf node due to flawed global state management during traversal.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Printing 'count' here is wrong because 'count' does not correctly reflect the height of the current leaf node due to flawed global state management during traversal.]

29.reason: [Incrementing a global 'count' before recursing assumes both subtrees share the same depth, which breaks when backtracking; this leads to incorrect height values for leaves in different branches.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Incrementing a global 'count' before recursing assumes both subtrees share the same depth, which breaks when backtracking; this leads to incorrect height values for leaves in different branches.]

32.reason: [Decrementing global 'count' after recursion fails to correctly restore height context for sibling or parent nodes, causing inconsistent and incorrect height tracking.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Decrementing global 'count' after recursion fails to correctly restore height context for sibling or parent nodes, causing inconsistent and incorrect height tracking.]

44.reason: [The function leafprint does not handle the case when root is NULL, leading to undefined behavior (dereferencing NULL pointer). Also, the height tracking using a global 'count' variable is incorrect because it doesn't represent the actual depth of the current node during traversal.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The function leafprint does not handle the case when root is NULL, leading to undefined behavior (dereferencing NULL pointer). Also, the height tracking using a global 'count' variable is incorrect because it doesn't represent the actual depth of the current node during traversal.]
